title: moraea aurantiaca
intro: Moraea aurantiaca, also known as the orange-flowered gladiolus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae. Traditionally, the plant's roots and tubers have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Moraea aurantiaca include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems. The plant's herbal preparations typically involve boiling the roots and tubers in water to create a decoction, which can be consumed as a tea or used topically as a poultice.
